item.title: 5 years restoring a formerly vacant 1927 Detroit home. The inside of the home was complete in 2018 and now we have moved to the exterior of the property with the build of a new garage and sunroom that are period appropriate for the 1920s.
item.description:
Exterior before view our entire story at www.between6and7.com
Exterior after restoration of original windows, new landscaping and new paint
Kitchen and breakfast nook before
Kitchen and breakfast nook after
Before
During
After
Dining before
Dining after
Stairs before
Stairs after
Master bedroom before
Master bedroom after
Bedroom converted into master bath
Bedroom converted into master bath
Kitchen before
Kitchen after
Guest bath before
Bath after
Living room before
Living room after
Powder room before
Powder room after
Beginning guild of sunroom
Sunroom framing
Garage is framed and sunroom framing began
The plan
Leaded windows being installed in sunroom
Leaded windows being installed in sunroom
Leaded windows being installed in sunroom
Beadboard Install in garage
Electrical rough in sunroom
Insulation day
Insulation day
Beadboard garage install
Beadboard garage install
Beadboard garage install
Window install complete
Window install compelte
Skylight install
Skylight install
Skylight install
Skylight install
We still have a long way to go! I post our progress on Instagram @between6and7 and keep a construction diary at www.between6and7.com

item.title: The Honor Walk
item.description:
Jarod Markus-Brooks. Born June 27th, 1998. Died February 11th, 2022. Aged 23 years. February 14th 2022, 1am, John Muir Medical Center, Walnut Creek, California. The photo attached is known as an "Honor Walk." It is often performed in hospitals when an organ donor, such as Jarod, offers his own body for the life of others. This was Jarod's Honor Walk, and is the last time I will ever see my son. Although I think it was edited for time, these were the words I composed for the OR team to read aloud prior to their important task. "For those of you in the OR right now: Like my son, you are all blessed. You are all beautiful. You are all essential. You have before you the body of my son. He was also blessed. He was also beautiful and to so many people, he was essential. I could say to you: 'The candle that burns twice as bright lasts half as long,' but Jarod was not one to appreciate cliches. Instead, I will say that his passion, his empathy and his fierce ability to love will live on far past his death through your hands. When you are finished here, this important work, I would appreciate if you would do the following, in his honor: Please. Please call your children, your parents, your wife or your husband or your partner. Please tell them know how much you love them. How important they are to you. How you, above so many others, understand how fragile life is, but also how eternal love is. He would want this because he would often put others above himself. Embolden yourselves to live as Jarod did; unapologetically. While the Pandemic raged across the world, you were the sentinels, as Jarod, my son, my baby boy, was the sentinel and beacon of light to so many people in his life. This is our charge, our purpose and our meaning, right now. This moment. This unrecoverable speck of time. Because all moments are blessed. All moments are beautiful. All moments are essential. And for Jarod, I want to say, my love for you is eternal. You are my son. I don’t know where you are headed but I do know that you will spread love, laughter, compassion, empathy and service to whoever you meet along the way. For me, personally, I am gutted. I am wrecked. I am without comprehension. This is my son. My Light and my Life. I love him with every fiber of this mortal existence. I always knew that he was destined for great things. What I did not know is that great things meant sacrificing his life on this Earth. Go in peace, my son. Go with God. Go with the Eternal. Even though you are not with us physically, your Soul lives on. You are now set to do what you have always wanted to do: Help another. Bless you, my boy. 'You can finally rest.'" So many people have reached out to me to show support. Many have asked if there is some gift they could give in Jarod's name. The most important gift I know he would want is what I've always said- take a moment to reach out to your loved ones, because time is far too short and far too fragile. If, however, you are interested in making a small donation in Jarod's name (Jarod Markus-Brooks): For my healthcare friends, consider donating to Alameda Healthcare Foundation http://www.alamedahealthsystem.org/giving/ . AHS is where I found my purpose in healthcare and public service. They are the reason I do what I do. If you are faith based, consider sending a small gift to the Pastor's Fund at Christ Episcopal Church in Alameda, CA https://christchurchalameda.org/ . Pastor Stephen McHale stood by me, my family and my son in his final moments. To all my Aggies out there, consider the UC Davis Student Scholars Fund https://give.ucdavis.edu/UGSH/322613 . Jarod was passionate about UCD and was always, always willing to help other students. It is fitting that Jarod gave gifts of life on February 14th, 2022. Not only is it Valentine's Day, but it also happens to be National Donor Day. Donating his organs on National Donor Day is definitely Jarod's style. If he was going to do something, he did it all the way. Although he may not be with us in life anymore, he definitely won life. And remember, tell your loved ones you love them, every day. Forgive the small things. Work to heal the old wounds. Be compassionate. Be kind. Come from a place of love. Because, isn't that the greatest gift and the greatest way to honor those that went before us?
Dog Tax: To all my imgurians- THANK YOU FOR YOUR COMPASSION, YOUR LOVE AND YOUR SUPPORT. It has not gone unnoticed. This is Maisie. She makes us smile in the darkest times. Please smile along with her.
item.dc:creator: aceebrofb

item.title: More please.
item.dc:creator: ExistenceIsPainWasTaken

item.title: That was unexpected
item.description: A little over 2 years ago I grew unhappy, left my wife and went to back to my home state. I went through depression, very hard times and was miserable for most of it. The only thing that kept me going was that I had custody of my son from my first relationship. I worked an okay job that paid the bills and took care of my son. He kept me going and spending time with him made me happy. Long story short, me ex wife of course found a boyfriend within the time we were separate. I was with others in the mean time too but not lasting due to my issues. I eventually realized my son was better with her so I let him go live with her and he has been doing much better. I thought I would go back into depression but I got a very high paying job, been paying off debt. All at the advice of my ex wife. I eventually realized it was a mistake for me to leave. I started taking my ex wife advice which has been benefiting me. I've been getting happier and doing better all around. I asked her to be my valentine and she said yes. I asked her about the boyfriend she had and they had issues where she decided to leave him. I ordered flowers to be delivered to her and we had an awesome video dinner date. We talked for hours about everything. I admitted I was stupid for leaving and not listening. My life was better with her and she agrees hers was better with me. Once I fix my life completely I may just end up going back to my ex wife because she truly completes me and deserves the better me I've become. And it is all due to her always being by my side even when I was at my worst. Tldr: asked ex wife to be my valentine, she said yes so I had flowers delivered to her along with a dinner so we could have a video dinner. It went awesome and we are falling back in love with each other.
item.dc:creator: L4dead2

item.title: It is a shame people tried to cheat her.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Designed a bit of a daring dress for V-Day!
item.description:
Does this look deceptively simple? Yeah, I guess it does a bit. This was a little bit of a challenge to myself to work my patternmatching skills. I drafted this pattern using my bodice block. The bodice pattern just has 3 pieces—the neckline, the side front and the side back! The skirt is 4x quarter circle pieces to create those sweet chevrons.
The amazing thing about the bodice is that the neckline pattern piece is one straight, simple piece that goes from front to back (mirrored on both sides and matched down the middle). So there is no shoulder seam! The challenge here was preventing it from gaping and connecting the two sides at an angle that wasn’t too far off the shoulders and not cut too low in the front or the back. Geometry gone wild, y’all.
The back v is low but sits just above my bra which I’m really happy about because I’ve never been a fan of designs where you have to be braless for it to work. The invisible zipper was super challenging to match stripes on both sides. I kind of wish I painted the pull red with a little nail polish but it’s fine for now. My secret to a good zipper? Fusible hem tape to act as interfacing. Interfacing sucks and the hem tape works SO much better, my god. Anyways, I hemmed the skirt with a bias tape facing (AFTER trimming a few days after hanging it). Always gotta hang your bias skirts before hemming, don’t skip that.
The neckline on the front is pretty daring for me but I’m glad the design didn’t force it to be even lower than that! I think on the next iteration if I make this again, I will pull up the shoulders about a centimetre to firm up the neckline a little. We’re all learning as we go, nothing is ever as perfect as it might look!
As you can see a bit better from this picture, the skirt is made up of 4 pieces and is pattern matched along the front, back, and even the side seams. I just love the chevrons! Definitely planning a rainbow pride dress using this pattern one day when I can afford 5 more metres of cotton drill fabric. I sewed this over just about 4 livestreams, including the test run! I stream my work 4 times per week, a lot of patternmaking and some cross stitch and miscellaneous stuff thrown in. If you wanna watch me sewing stuff live, here is a shameless plug: http://twitch.tv/carbonchic
item.dc:creator: CarbonChic
